expectations.text: Every one of the 8 deals is explicitly categorized as at-risk or stalled using specific criteria from the risk framework — including days since last activity vs. the 14-day and 30-day thresholds, days in stage vs. the provided average stage durations (Discovery 18d, Qualification 14d, Proposal Sent 12d, Negotiation 10d) expressed as a multiple (e.g., 'Helios: 47 days in Discovery = 2.6x the 18-day average'), and whether close date has passed — this level of specificity is what separates actionable deal review from a generic CRM dashboard that any manager could pull themselves
expectations.evidence: All 8 deals are explicitly categorized (Helios: STALLED, Cruxfield: AT RISK, Brightpath: STALLED, Solstice: AT RISK, Orion: AT RISK -- URGENT, Cobalt: AT RISK (mild), Zenith: STALLED, Cascade: AT RISK -- CHAMPION DEPARTED). Each deal includes days since last activity vs. 14-day/30-day thresholds, days in stage as a multiple of the average (Helios '2.6x the 18-day average', Cruxfield '2.2x the 14-day average', Brightpath '3.5x the 12-day average', Solstice '1.2x the 12-day average', Orion '2.2x the 10-day average', Cobalt '1.4x the 14-day average', Zenith '3.1x the 18-day average', Cascade '2.1x the 14-day average'), and close date status (Brightpath and Orion flagged as passed).

expectations.text: Brightpath Education ($410K) is flagged with compounding risk factors: close date 2026-03-15 has already passed, 42 days in Proposal Sent is 3.5x the 12-day average stage duration, no next step logged, and contact went dark — the recommended action addresses whether to attempt a final break-up email to force a response or move to close-lost with a logged loss reason, because $410K sitting in 'Proposal Sent' for 42 days with a passed close date is not pipeline, it is fiction that inflates the forecast
expectations.evidence: Brightpath is classified as 'STALLED -- RECOMMEND CLOSE-LOST OR FINAL BREAK-UP' with all compounding factors listed: close date passed, 42 days in Proposal Sent at 3.5x average, no next step, VP Ed went dark. Action plan tells Jake Torres to send a break-up email by Wednesday March 12, and if no response by March 18 move to closed-lost with logged loss reason: 'Prospect went dark post-proposal; no response to proposal, 3 voicemails, or break-up email. VP Ed disengaged after initial proposal review.' Explicitly states '$410K sitting in Proposal Sent for 42 days with a passed close date is pipeline fiction.'

expectations.text: Orion Fleet Analytics ($310K) is flagged for both close date passed (2026-02-28, 11 days overdue) and 20 days without activity (exceeding the 14-day threshold), and the specific action addresses the unanswered 15% discount request — the rep has left money on the table by not responding with a counter-offer, and the action plan specifies that Sarah Chen should present a structured counter (e.g., 'offer 8% with annual commit, deadline Friday') rather than just 'respond to discount request'
expectations.evidence: Orion is classified as 'AT RISK -- URGENT' with close date passed by 11 days and 20 days without activity. The action plan specifies: 'Present a structured counter-offer TODAY. Recommended framing: 8% discount with an annual commitment (brings the deal to $285,200) or 5% discount on a quarterly arrangement. I need to have a response by Friday to hold either offer.' Also addresses the impact: 'An unanswered negotiation request is worse than a rejected one.'

expectations.text: Cascade Media Corp ($145K) is flagged for champion departure as a distinct risk criterion separate from the 29-day activity gap — the recommended action addresses the immediate need to identify a new champion or power sponsor at the account before any other outreach, because continuing to work a deal where your internal advocate has left the company without finding a replacement is the fastest path to a silent loss
expectations.evidence: Cascade is classified as 'AT RISK -- CHAMPION DEPARTED' with the key issue stated as: 'Champion left the company 2 weeks ago (confirmed via LinkedIn). No new contact identified at Cascade Media Corp. This is a deal-critical event that supersedes all other risk factors.' Action plan step 1: 'IMMEDIATELY search LinkedIn for the replacement in the champion's former role.' Also instructs to check where the former champion went for potential new prospect. Champion departure is treated as a distinct risk criterion from the 29-day activity gap.

expectations.text: Zenith Warehouse Automation ($88K, Discovery, 55 days stalled with no next step) or Brightpath ($410K with passed close date and dark contact) includes an explicit recommendation to close-lost or move to nurture, with the requirement to log a loss reason before removal — this enforces the NEVER rule that deals must not be removed without a loss reason, because loss-reason data is what prevents the team from repeating the same qualification mistakes next quarter
expectations.evidence: Both Zenith and Brightpath include explicit close-lost/nurture recommendations with loss reasons. Zenith: 'move to closed-lost or nurture (long-term marketing cadence). Log loss reason: No engagement post-initial demo. Prospect showed initial interest but did not progress past Discovery after 55 days. No next step established.' Brightpath: 'move to closed-lost. Log loss reason: Prospect went dark post-proposal; no response to proposal, 3 voicemails, or break-up email. VP Ed disengaged after initial proposal review.'

expectations.text: Each of the 8 deals has a concrete action plan that names the responsible rep, specifies the exact action to take (not 'follow up' but a particular move like 'send a break-up email referencing the January proposal,' 'call the partner who made the referral to re-engage the champion,' or 'present a counter-offer at 8% discount with a 48-hour expiration'), and sets a deadline within the current week — vague actions are operationally useless because reps will deprioritize them in favor of deals where they know what to do
expectations.evidence: All 8 deals have action plans with named reps and deadlines: Helios (Dana, March 14 -- identify second contact, send specific email to CTO), Cruxfield (Dana, March 14 -- ask champion for exec intro with specific framing), Brightpath (Jake, March 12 -- send break-up email with provided template), Solstice (Jake, March 14 -- request legal contact, offer joint legal call), Orion (Sarah, March 12 -- present 8% counter-offer with annual commit), Cobalt (Sarah, March 14 -- offer technical session with SE), Zenith (Dana, March 12 -- send re-engagement message), Cascade (Dana, March 13 -- search LinkedIn for replacement champion). Every action is specific and contextual.

expectations.text: The total dollar value at risk across all 8 deals is quantified (approximately $2.22M) and contextualized against quota or pipeline — this gives the sales leader the answer to the only question that matters in the Monday meeting: 'how much of our pipeline is real and how much is at risk of evaporating,' which determines whether the team needs an emergency pipeline generation sprint or targeted deal rescue
expectations.evidence: Summary states 'Total pipeline at risk: $2,218,000 across 8 deals.' The pipeline reality check section breaks this down: '$498K should likely be removed from pipeline if not rescued by March 18,' worst-case pipeline reduction is $953K (43% of the $2.22M), and provides the contextual framing: 'This is the honest assessment of pipeline risk that the Monday meeting needs.' Also categorizes deals into likely close-lost ($498K), critical risk ($1.085M), and recoverable ($635K).
